---
title: "Widget-Level Parameter Schemas"
---

## Overview

`DeepLearningParamSchemas.hpp` defines **widget-level** reflect-cpp parameter structs
and tagged unions that compose the per-component param structs from the DeepLearning
library. These types describe how the UI organizes slot bindings and are separate from
the core inference library.

**Location:** `src/WhiskerToolbox/DeepLearning_Widget/Core/DeepLearningParamSchemas.hpp`

`ParameterUIHints` declarations for schema extraction:
`src/WhiskerToolbox/DeepLearning_Widget/Core/DeepLearningParamSchemasUIHints.hpp`
(definitions in `DeepLearningParamSchemas.cpp`).

## Design Rationale

Phase 0.0 of the DeepLearning Widget refactoring moved per-component parameter structs
(e.g. `MaskDecoderParams`, `Point2DEncoderParams`) into the DeepLearning library. This
phase (0.1) defines the **widget-level binding structs** that compose those library
types into variant hierarchies representing UI choices.

The key distinction:

| Layer | Owns | Example |
|---|---|---|
| DeepLearning library | Per-component params (one struct per encoder/decoder/module) | `dl::MaskDecoderParams{threshold}` |
| Widget level | Variant unions and composite structs for UI binding | `dl::widget::DecoderVariant` (tagged union of all decoder params) |

## Tagged Unions

All variants use `rfl::TaggedUnion` with a discriminator field name. The
`extractParameterSchema<>()` function auto-detects these and produces variant field
descriptors with sub-schemas for each alternative.

### CaptureModeVariant

Discriminator: `"capture_mode"`

How a static input is acquired:

- **`RelativeCaptureParams`** — Encode at `current_frame + time_offset` each run.
- **`AbsoluteCaptureParams`** — Encode once at a specific frame and cache the tensor.

### RecurrentInitVariant

Discriminator: `"init_mode"`

How recurrent (feedback) state is initialized at t=0:

- **`ZerosInitParams`** — Initialize with zeros (no fields).
- **`StaticCaptureInitParams`** — Capture a static data source (`data_key`, `frame`).
- **`FirstOutputInitParams`** — Use the first model output (no fields).

### SequenceEntryVariant

Discriminator: `"source_type"`

One entry in a sequence (static) input slot:

- **`StaticSequenceEntryParams`** — Data-driven entry (`data_key`, `capture_mode_str`, `time_offset`).
- **`RecurrentSequenceEntryParams`** — Recurrent feedback entry (`output_slot_name`, `init_mode_str`).

### PostEncoderVariant

Discriminator: `"module"`

Which post-encoder module to apply:

- **`NoPostEncoderParams`** — Identity pass-through (no fields).
- **`dl::GlobalAvgPoolModuleParams`** — Global average pooling (no fields, re-used from library).
- **`dl::SpatialPointModuleParams`** — Spatial point extraction (`interpolation` enum, re-used from library).

### DecoderVariant

Discriminator: `"decoder"`

Which decoder to use for an output slot. All alternatives are re-used from the
DeepLearning library:

- **`dl::MaskDecoderParams`** — `{threshold}`
- **`dl::PointDecoderParams`** — `{subpixel, threshold}`
- **`dl::LineDecoderParams`** — `{threshold}`
- **`dl::FeatureVectorDecoderParams`** — (no fields)

## Composite Structs

### OutputSlotParams

Combines a `data_key` (DataManager target for decoded results) with a `DecoderVariant`:

```cpp
struct OutputSlotParams {
    std::string data_key;
    DecoderVariant decoder = MaskDecoderParams{};
};
```

### EncoderShapeParams

Custom encoder input/output dimensions (UI-only):

```cpp
struct EncoderShapeParams {
    rfl::Validator<int, rfl::Minimum<1>> input_height = 224;
    rfl::Validator<int, rfl::Minimum<1>> input_width = 224;
    std::string output_shape;
};
```

## ParameterUIHints

Implementations live in `DeepLearningParamSchemas.cpp`. Explicit specializations of
`ParameterUIHints<dl::widget::…>` must also be **declared** in
`DeepLearningParamSchemasUIHints.hpp`. Any translation unit that calls
`extractParameterSchema<T>()` for these widget types must include that header;
otherwise the compiler only sees the primary `ParameterUIHints` template and
instantiates its empty `annotate`, so tooltips and other hints are missing at runtime.

Specializations are provided for structs that benefit from UI annotations:

| Struct | Annotations |
|---|---|
| `RelativeCaptureParams` | Tooltip for `time_offset` |
| `StaticCaptureInitParams` | Tooltips for `data_key`, `frame` |
| `StaticSequenceEntryParams` | Display name + allowed values for `capture_mode_str` |
| `RecurrentSequenceEntryParams` | Display name + allowed values for `init_mode_str` |
| `OutputSlotParams` | Tooltips for `data_key`, `decoder` |
| `EncoderShapeParams` | Tooltips for all three fields |

## Test Coverage

Unit tests live in `tests/DeepLearning/param_schemas/DeepLearningParamSchemas.test.cpp`
and verify:

- Schema extraction for all variant types (correct discriminators, alternatives, sub-schema fields)
- JSON round-trips via `rfl::json::write` / `rfl::json::read` for every variant alternative
- UIHints annotations (display names, allowed values, tooltips)
- Composite struct schema extraction (`OutputSlotParams`, `EncoderShapeParams`)
